name: Hash Array Mapped Trie (HAMT)

x.invariant: a trie keyed on slices of the hash; each node holds a bitmap + a packed array of only the populated children.
x.memoryOverhead: Bitmap + compact child array per node; structural sharing keeps update cost to one root-to-leaf path.
x.locality: Shallow (radix 32) so few indirections, but pointer-chasing between nodes.
x.mutationModel: Persistent / immutable: updates return a new root sharing all untouched subtrees; the old version stays valid.
x.concurrency: Immutable versions are trivially safe to share across threads; transient (mutable) builders speed up bulk loads.

def: A persistent map/set implemented as a wide trie over key hashes, giving near-O(1) lookups with cheap immutable updates via structural sharing.
problem: An immutable map that can be "updated" cheaply without copying the whole structure.
mechanism: Consumes the key hash 5 bits at a time to index a 32-way node; a per-node bitmap stores only populated slots compactly. Updates copy only the path from root to the changed leaf.
benefits: Persistent: O(1)-ish copy with structural sharing
limits: Higher constants than a mutable hash table
failures: Poor hash distribution deepens collision chains
whenNot: Single-threaded mutable workload where a plain hash table wins
myths: "Immutable means slow" — sharing makes updates cheap, not full copies
